research.status_summary: Demostack appears operational and modestly active as of mid-2026, with no signals of acquisition, layoffs, or shutdown — headcount sits at ~59 employees, down from a peak of ~93 at its 2022 Series B.
research.hiring_policy: Demostack lists open roles on its careers page and ZoomInfo notes an active Tel Aviv-based Technical Product Manager search, suggesting an Israel R&D presence; no public evidence of visa sponsorship or explicit EOR-friendly policy for international candidates was found.
recent_news: Last confirmed funding is the $34M Series B (April 2022, Tiger Global + Bessemer), with no new round publicly announced through June 2026 — over 4 years since last raise.
Headcount has contracted from ~93 (2022) to ~59 as of April 30, 2026, per Tracxn — a ~37% reduction with no public layoff announcement.
Demostack joined the HubSpot App Marketplace (noted in April 2025 CBInsights update) and previously launched an AI Data Generator (Dec 2023); no major product announcements in Apr–Jun 2026.
CBInsights ranked Demostack as an 'Outperformer' in the interactive demo platforms ESP matrix, alongside Arcade, Consensus, and Instruqt.
CEO Jonathan Friedman recognized on a 'Top 25 Sales Enablement Software Companies of 2025' list per ZoomInfo data.
research.growth_signal: Flat-to-declining trajectory: headcount has shrunk ~37% since the 2022 Series B peak, no new funding in 4+ years, and the demo-automation space is crowding with better-funded rivals like Storylane and Navattic.
research.fit_for_lior: Weak fit — the company is a niche B2B sales demo tool in a contracting headcount phase with no AI-native product thread, no recent funding, and limited alignment with Lior's Creative AI, Product Analyst, or Vibe Coder positioning.
